text.title: aws-networking Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for aws-networking as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Single NAT Gateway shared across multiple AZs to reduce cost.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A shared NAT Gateway creates a single point of failure. If the NAT Gateway's AZ fails, all private subnets in other AZs lose internet egress even though those AZs are still operational.

failure-mode.description: Security groups with 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on ports other than 80/443 on ALB.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every open inbound rule is an active attack surface. SSH (22), RDP (3389), databases (5432, 3306), and admin interfaces exposed to the internet are continuously probed by automated scanners.

failure-mode.description: Assuming VPC Peering is transitive for multi-hop connectivity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: VPC Peering is point-to-point only. Traffic cannot pass through a peered VPC to reach a third VPC. Transit Gateway or VPC Lattice is required for hub-and-spoke topologies.
